title: [CI:BUILD] Combine buildahimage Containerfiles
body:
<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read and understood our contributing guidelines
(https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md) as well as ensuring
that all your commits are signed with `git commit -s`.
-->

#### What type of PR is this?

<!--
Please label this pull request according to what type of issue you are
addressing, especially if this is a release targeted pull request.

Uncomment only one `/kind <>` line, hit enter to put that in a new line, and
remove leading whitespace from that line:
-->
/kind cleanup

#### What this PR does / why we need it:
A single `Containerfile` should be easier to maintain and more reliable than having three nearly identical files. The new file uses an argument named `RELEASE` set to one of: `stable`, `testing`, or `upstream`.

#### How to verify it
Build the containerfile with a RELEASE build parameter set. Something like:
```bash
buildah bud -t buildah:test-build --build-arg "RELEASE=upstream" contrib/buildahimage
```
#### Which issue(s) this PR fixes:
None
#### Special notes for your reviewer:
This will break the CI builder. I believe the image builder script combines the build context path and the image version into a single parameter, using basename on the path to get the version/subimage name. That would need updating to build this properly. But given how many mistakes and inconsistencies these files had, I think it may make maintenance easier.

As far as I can tell, the relevant files would be `.cirrus.yml` in this repository and https://github.com/containers/automation_images/blob/main/build-push/bin/main.sh. Since that is a multi-repository change involving the CI system, I haven't attempted it in this pull request. I'm new to such things.

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes please follow the kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
None
```

comments.body:
LGTM
I will wait for @cevich to respond, since I think his builders will need to be modified.
/approve

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *AndASM*, *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fbuildah). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

> I will wait for @cevich to respond, since I think his builders will need to be modified.

Thanks Dan.  This will most certainly break the build automation.  Though the changes seem correct on quick inspection, we def. don't want to merge this until [the automation is fixed.](https://github.com/containers/automation_images/blob/main/build-push/bin/main.sh)  I'll need to find my thinking-cap to figure out a solution.

FYI- @AndASM I'm going to update the PR description with a magic keyword.  If you rebase/force-push to this PR, you'll see three image-build test tasks pop up.  They can be manually triggered to do a test-run of our automation (which will fail).  This will be helpful once I figure out how to update our build script.
/hold
Thanks @AndASM I'm working on updates to the build script, which we can test here shortly (assuming I can manage to spell *flavour* correctly lol).
OMG :rofl: I am most definitely the befouddled one.  Too funny.  Fair enough, I'll adjust the build script to please my spell-checker lol.

> OMG 🤣 I am most definitely the befouddled one. Too funny. Fair enough, I'll adjust the build script to please my spell-checker lol.

When I decided to (finally) use my real name on my Github account so I could contribute to projects like this I created a new domain to start housing any personal coding projects or related online artifacts. I used it for my sign-off email address. I just HAD to make a joke using it. Thank you for giving me the opportunity to! 🤣

We can both be befuddled together! Everyone 'wins'!

Well I certainly appreciate both the humour and the contribution :grinning: 

Build-script update: I'm working on adding test to verify the new build-arg support.  Assuming that passes, as soon as I merge https://github.com/containers/automation_images/pull/213 we can trigger the test-builds here to confirm all the planets are properly aligned, and the automation $DIETYS still look favourably on us.

@AndASM I updated the build script to support passing the flavour in by argument.  Would you mind:

```diff
diff --git a/.cirrus.yml b/.cirrus.yml
index 114ee03ce..7b7bd72c4 100644
--- a/.cirrus.yml
+++ b/.cirrus.yml
@@ -322,7 +322,7 @@ in_podman_task:


 image_build_task: &image-build
-    name: "Build multi-arch $CTXDIR"
+    name: "Build multi-arch $FLAVOR"
     alias: image_build
     # Some of these container images take > 1h to build, limit
     # this task to a specific Cirrus-Cron entry with this name.
@@ -337,11 +337,11 @@ image_build_task: &image-build
         type: "n2-standard-4"
     matrix:
         - env:
-            CTXDIR: contrib/buildahimage/upstream
+            FLAVOR: upstream
         - env:
-            CTXDIR: contrib/buildahimage/testing
+            FLAVOR: testing
         - env:
-            CTXDIR: contrib/buildahimage/stable
+            FLAVOR: stable
     env:
         DISTRO_NV: "${FEDORA_NAME}"  # Required for repo cache extraction
         BUILDAH_USERNAME: ENCRYPTED[70e1d4f026cba5d82fc067944baab10f7c71c64bb6b75fce4eeb5c106694b3bbc8e08f8a1b926d6e03e85cf4e21833bb]
@@ -350,7 +350,7 @@ image_build_task: &image-build
         CONTAINERS_PASSWORD: ENCRYPTED[886cf4cc126e50b2fd7f2792235a22bb79e4b81db43f803a6214a38d3fd6c04cd4e64570b562cb32b04e5fbc435404b6]
     main_script:
         - source /etc/automation_environment
-        - main.sh $CIRRUS_REPO_CLONE_URL $CTXDIR
+        - main.sh $CIRRUS_REPO_CLONE_URL contrib/buildahimage $FLAVOR


 test_image_build_task:
```

Then :crossed_fingers: we can try triggering the test build tasks in this PR to experience the fail^H^H^H^Hglory.

> Then 🤞 we can try triggering the test build tasks in this PR to experience the fail^H^H^H^Hglory.

@cevich I added your changes and rebased on the latest main branch. We shall see about this glory 😆

LGTM
@cevich PTAL
I just triggered the test jobs, let's see what happens...
...I see in the output, it's properly passing `--build-arg FLAVOR=stable` so that's a good sign...
...great, it's building the images and manifest-list (this is going to take a while).  I'll check back in a few hours...
...yay :tada:

This LGTM with [one small change](https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/4300#discussion_r990333466), and it seems github wants you to rebase anyway.  Thanks for all your work on this, I'm glad we were able to get it working!

Accepted [the change](https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/4300#discussion_r990333466) from @cevich, and rebased to current. It looks like the automatic checks have kicked off.

Is there anything else pending for the hold on this? Let me know!
Nope, we're good.  I just triggered the test-build jobs.  Assuming they go green we can merge this.  Thanks again!

/lgtm
/hold
/hold cancel
